compiled_state.title: As an agent, I am guided to record suggestions when encountering friction during normal work
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update using-lockpick guidance to instruct agents to call suggestion-record.sh when they encounter repeated friction, unclear instructions, or workarounds
**Why**: The agent-driven capture path provides qualitative observations with full context — the agent knows exactly what went wrong, which file, and what it tried. This gives agents a constructive action when stuck instead of escalating or giving up
**Scope**:
- IN: Add a section to using-lockpick/SKILL.md explaining when and how to record suggestions, the suggestion-record.sh invocation pattern via the dso shim, and framing as advisory guidance (not a hard threshold)
- OUT: Stop hook integration (Story 3), PostToolUse hook (Story 4), retro consumption (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, using-lockpick guidance contains a section that instructs agents to record suggestions when encountering repeated friction, unclear instructions, or workarounds
  ← Satisfies: "using-lockpick guidance instructs agents to record suggestions when encountering repeated friction"
- When this story is complete, the guidance frames suggestion recording as advisory (not a hard threshold) and as a constructive alternative to escalation or giving up
  ← Satisfies: "framing it as advisory guidance, not a hard threshold"
- When this story is complete, the guidance includes the correct shim invocation pattern (.claude/scripts/dso suggestion-record.sh) so agents in any environment (worktree or main) can call it correctly
  ← Satisfies: "using-lockpick guidance instructs agents to record suggestions"
